original: Andrea Schone skates ladies world record 3 km (4:20.91)
The Wright brothers 1st file a patent for a flying machine, which is granted 3 years later
Nicaraguan National Literary Crusade begins: crusade teachers swear in plazas across the country to exterminate ignorance in just five months (will successfully reduce illiteracy from 50% to 12%)
A Sahara sandstorm turns snow in Sochi, Russia, orange in one of the largest-ever transfers of desert sand
Patrick Henryproclaims "Give me liberty or give me death" in a speech encouraging Virginia troops to join the American Revolutionary War
NHL New York IslandersMike Bossy's 20th career hat trick, in his 5th career 4-goal game, an 8-1 win over visiting Washington Capitols
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Old Dominion beat Tennessee 68-53 at Mount Pleasant
Wayne Gretzkysets NHL record with 802 goals scored
After some initial hesitation,Emma Watsonsigns on to appear as Hermione in the final three Harry Potter films
Steve Redgrave, British rower (5 Olympic gold medals 1984-2000) world's most successful male rower, born in Marlow
18-year-old English chemist William Perkin accidentally produces the first synthetic aniline dye ‘mauveine’ (purple) during his Easter holiday
Alick Wickham dives 200' into Australia's Yarra River
Space Shuttle Discovery moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 51-D mission
John William Drapertakes 1st successful photo in US of the Moon (daguerreotype), in New York City
Emmy Noether, German mathematician known for her contributions to abstract algebra, born in Erlangen, German Empire (d. 1935)[1][2]
Jim Trelease, American educator and author who stressed the importance of reading aloud to children (The Read-Aloud Handbook), born in Orange, New Jersey (d. 2022)[1][2][3]
John Cunningham sets world altitude record (54,492' (18,133 m))
Geoge Harrison and Friends' "The Concert for Bangladesh" concert film, directed by Saul Swimmer released in the US
Bette Nesmith Graham, American inventor (liquid paper), born in Dallas, Texas (d. 1980)
WHO says the COVID-19 pandemic is accelerating, 1st 100,000 cases took 67 days, 2nd 100,000 cases 11 days, 3rd 100,000 cases 4 days
Cargo ship Ever Given gets stuck in the Suez Canal, Egypt, a 400m megaship it completely blocks the shipping canal
Encarnación Alzona, pioneering Filipino historian, educator, and first Filipino woman to obtain a Ph.D., born in Biñan, Philippines (d. 2001)
Robert Gallo, American physician who co-discovered the AIDS virus and pioneered the HIV blood test, born in Waterbury, Connecticut[1][2]
1st airplane lands at the US Capitol in Washington, D.C.
Frank Silver and Irving Conn publish their hit song "Yes, We Have No Bananas"
NY Rangers blow 6-2 lead, lose 7-6 to Chicago Black Hawks; Bill Mosienko scores fastest hat trick in NHL history, 21 seconds
Nawab of Pataudi captains the Indian cricket team against the West Indies at the age of 21 years and 77 days
NASA launches Gemini 3, 1st US 2-man space flight (Gus Grissom & Young)
2 Utah scientists claim they have produced fusion at room temperature
The Russian Mir space station is disposed of, breaking up in the atmosphere before falling into the southern Pacific Ocean near Fiji
After 114 consecutive weeks as world #1 female tennis player, 25-year-old Australian Ash Barty makes shock retirement announcement; cites contentment with all she achieved in the sport
"In His Own Write", a book of short stories, poems and drawings byJohn Lennonis published

David Bowieis special surprise guest atTina Turnerconcert at NEC arena in Birmingham, England; they perform duets of Bowie's "Tonight" and a medley of Chris Montez' "Let's Dance" and Bowie's song of the same name
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Wanderers Stadium, Johannesburg, South Africa: Australia retain trophy with 125 run win over India; Player of the Match:Ricky Ponting(AUS) 140 no (121)
John Bartram, naturalist and explorer, father of American botany, born near Darby, Pennsylvania (d. 1777)
Pierre-Simon, marquis de Laplace, French mathematician, astronomer and physicist (stability of the solar system), born in Beaumount-en-Auge, Normandy, France (d. 1827)
William Smith, British geologist who is credited with creating the first nationwide geological map (Strata Identified by Organized Fossils), born in Churchill, Oxfordshire (d. 1839)
Fannie Farmer, American culinary pioneer who revolutionised modern cooking through the introduction of precise measurements (Boston Cooking-School Cook Book), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1915)
Hermann Staudinger, German chemist and plastics researcher (Nobel Prize for Chemistry,1953 for polymers), born in Worms, Germany (d. 1965)[1]
Dave Frishberg, American jazz pianist, vocalist, composer, and lyricist ("I'm Just A Bill"; "Van Lingle Mungo"), born in St. Paul, Minnesota (d. 2021)
Robertas Žulpa, Lithuanian swimmer (Olympic gold USSR 200m breaststroke 1980), born in Vilnius, Lithuania (d. 2024)
GPR investigation ofShakespeare's tomb at Holy Trinity Church in Stratford concludes the Bard's skull probably has been stolen
Lithuania proclaims independence
Walt Disney Company launches premium cable Disney Junior channel, with 24-hour programming for the 2-7 year old audience
Larry HolmesTKOs Osvaldo Ocasio in 7 for heavyweight boxing title
Paul Grimault, French animator (is Le Roi et l'oiseau THe King and the Bird), born in Neuilly-sur-Seine, France (d. 1994)
Albert H Crews, American chemical and aeronautical engineer and USAF astronaut (X-20 Dyna-Soar program), born in El Dorado, Arkansas
Maynard Jackson, American lawyer and the first African American mayor of Atlanta (1974-82; 1990-94), born in Dallas, Texas (d. 2003)
Ric Ocasek [Otcasek], American songwriter, guitarist, singer, and producer (The Cars - "Best Friend's Girl"; "Shake It Up"; "You Are The Girl"), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 2019)
Kim Stanley Robinson, American sci-fi author (Mars Trilogy), born in Waukegan, Illinois
Damon Albarn, English rock singer-songwriter, and musician (Blur - "Girls & Boys"; "Country House"; Gorillaz - "Clint Eastwood"; "Feels Good, Inc."), born in Whitechapel, London
Elisha Otisinstalls his first elevator at 488 Broadway in New York City
18th recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
The ship John Wickliffe arrives at Port Chalmers carrying the first Scottish settlers for Dunedin, New Zealand. Otago province is founded.
UN World Meteorological Org established
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Immaculata beat Mississippi College 68-53 in Manhattan, Kansas
Abel Prize, mathematics' Nobel equivalent, awarded to American Dennis Sullivan for his work on topography
Utah is the first state to limit social media for minors, including an overnight curfew and parental consents
Polish generalTadeusz Kościuszkoreturns to Kraków, Poland to led the Kościuszko Uprising against Russian and Prussian control
Elvis Presleybegins his final concert tour
